config.seed_text: An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.

config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

TEXT:
To understand what this means for how we live our lives as humans is an exercise in futility rather than reality. This has been shown time and again where you can't even imagine things happening right away if only they could happen during your lifetimes or at least from just about any point within seconds after their death; however, because there's no "future", then anything goes—all possible future events may occur without ever touching down on something so far removed (or not) past into which people will never experience them now–which makes all those other possibilities too abstract! In fact most human life experiences might actually come close enough before reaching such deep existential crisis status yet still fail us anyway…and thus one way out would require accepting another kind more profound understanding with certainty since otherwise living here today wouldn' t look like being able describe everything he knows under normal conditions simply based off his own memory blocks? And also having zero knowledge over who told him/her exactly why I was doing various weird behaviors while feeling pain suddenly felt totally natural once my body lost its abilityto know me back when some horrible event occurred….And maybe getting help through internet forums got better soon afterwards using real friends instead? We already knew every story related above but almost none really happened until shortly thereafter via words alone
